episode_text:
day=4; meal=chicken nuggets, broccoli, and ketchup; setting=Social with person on Left; time_of_day=morning;
preferences: microwave_time=2 min; occlusion_relevance=minimize left occlusion; robot_speed=slow; skewering_axis=perpendicular to major axis; web_interface_confirmation=no; transfer_mode=inside mouth transfer; outside_mouth_distance=not applicable; convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transfer=speech + LED; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_feeding=open mouth; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_drinking=open mouth; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_wiping=autocontinue; convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transfer=speech + LED; detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding=perception; detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking=perception; detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping=perception; retract_between_bites=no; bite_dipping_preference=less; wait_before_autocontinue_seconds=100 sec

microwave_time.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND fruit meal or other foods intended to be served cold THEN no microwave BECAUSE the observed personal fruit meal used no reheating. IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND foods without sauces or dips that are not cold-served fruit/dessert THEN 1 min BECAUSE the earlier observed personal meal used light reheating. IF working context AND neutral affect AND bite-sized sandwiches or other warm savory foods THEN 2 min BECAUSE the working meal selected stronger reheating for that food type. IF social setting AND neutral affect AND warm savory foods such as chicken nuggets and vegetables THEN 2 min BECAUSE the social meal also selected stronger reheating.

occlusion_relevance.user_tendencies: IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the right AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE the user explicitly chose to protect visibility of the device on the right. IF social setting with a person on the left AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN minimize left occlusion BECAUSE the social meal explicitly prioritized keeping the left side visually open. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the left AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE the observed left-side working meal did not prioritize view protection. IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE the observed personal meal did not prioritize occlusion. Current evidence suggests occlusion concern is selective and tied to the location of a socially or functionally important target.

robot_speed.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND foods without sauces or dips THEN slow BECAUSE the observed personal meal used a slower pace. IF social setting AND neutral affect AND warm savory foods THEN slow BECAUSE the social meal also selected the slowest speed, suggesting comfort and social smoothness are prioritized there. IF working context AND neutral affect AND strong efficiency pressure is present THEN fast BECAUSE one working meal favored the fastest option. IF working context AND neutral affect AND bite-sized sandwiches THEN medium BECAUSE another working meal selected a moderate speed, indicating the user prefers more efficiency while working but not always the maximum speed.

skewering_axis.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND solid bite-sized foods in general THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE earlier observed meals used the reliability-oriented option. IF social setting AND neutral affect AND foods such as chicken nuggets and broccoli THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE the social meal also selected the reliability-oriented option. IF working context AND neutral affect AND bite-sized sandwiches THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE the working meal selected the narrower-bite option, suggesting food geometry can outweigh the broader preference for acquisition reliability.

web_interface_confirmation.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN no BECAUSE observed personal meals avoided extra confirmation, consistent with minimizing interaction burden. IF working context AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN no BECAUSE working meals also skipped confirmation while occupied. IF social setting AND neutral affect AND warm savory foods THEN no BECAUSE the social meal still avoided extra confirmation. No context-dependent variation observed yet.

transfer_mode.user_tendencies: IF any dining context AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user cannot lean forward due to lack of trunk control, making outside-mouth transfer physically unsuitable.

outside_mouth_distance.user_tendencies: IF any dining context AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN not applicable BECAUSE the user's physical abilities support inside mouth transfer rather than outside-mouth transfer.

convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transf.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the observed personal meal used multimodal signaling. IF social setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the social meal also used multimodal signaling, suggesting a preference for salient cues when another person is present. IF working context AND neutral affect AND laptop on the right THEN speech BECAUSE the earlier working meal used an auditory cue only. IF working context AND neutral affect AND laptop on the left THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the other working meal used multimodal cueing. Current evidence suggests multimodal cueing is the general preference, with a speech-only exception in one right-side working setup.

detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfe.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user has good mouth control and selected this low-effort, user-driven method in a personal setting. IF social setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN open mouth BECAUSE the social meal also used intentional mouth-based initiation despite conversational risk, indicating the user prefers preserving direct timing control over full automation in that context. IF working context AND neutral affect AND some meals favor stronger automation THEN autocontinue BECAUSE one working meal favored reduced interaction burden. IF working context AND neutral affect AND bite-sized sandwiches THEN open mouth BECAUSE another working meal returned to intentional user-driven initiation. Button input is incompatible with severe upper-limb paralysis. Across observed cases, when not clearly prioritizing efficiency, the user tends to favor low-effort but explicit control.

IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN open mouth BECAUSE the observed personal meal used this low-effort but intentional readiness signal for drinking. IF social setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN open mouth BECAUSE the social meal also used intentional mouth-based sip initiation. IF working context AND neutral affect AND some meals favor stronger automation THEN autocontinue BECAUSE one working meal favored faster automated flow. IF working context AND neutral affect AND bite-sized sandwiches THEN open mouth BECAUSE another working meal selected intentional user-driven sip initiation. Button input is incompatible with severe upper-limb paralysis. Across observed cases, when not clearly prioritizing efficiency, the user tends to favor low-effort but explicit control.

IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the observed personal meal used automation for wiping initiation. IF working context AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN autocontinue BECAUSE working meals also used automation. IF social setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the social meal did the same. Button input is incompatible with severe upper-limb paralysis. No context-dependent variation observed yet.

convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transf.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the observed personal meal used multimodal completion cues. IF social setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the social meal also used multimodal completion cues. IF working context AND neutral affect AND laptop on the right THEN speech BECAUSE the earlier working meal used auditory completion cueing only. IF working context AND neutral affect AND laptop on the left THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the other working meal used multimodal completion cueing. Current evidence suggests multimodal completion cueing is the general preference, with a speech-only exception in one right-side working setup.

detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding.user_tendencies: IF any dining context AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN perception BECAUSE the user consistently selected reliable sensor-based completion detection, and button input is incompatible with severe upper-limb paralysis.

detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking.user_tendencies: IF any dining context AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN perception BECAUSE the user has good head control and consistently selected perception-based completion for drinking; button input is incompatible with severe upper-limb paralysis.

detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping.user_tendencies: IF any dining context AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN perception BECAUSE the user has good head control and consistently selected perception-based completion for mouth wiping; button input is incompatible with severe upper-limb paralysis.

retract_between_bites.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN no BECAUSE the observed personal meal kept the robot in place between tasks to reduce meal time. IF working context AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN no BECAUSE working meals also prioritized efficiency over reducing visual obstruction. IF social setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN no BECAUSE the social meal still preferred not retracting despite left-side occlusion concerns. Current evidence suggests the user generally prioritizes shorter meal flow over retracting between tasks.

bite_dipping_preference.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND foods without dippable items or sauces THEN do not dip BECAUSE the observed personal fruit meal used no dipping. IF working context AND neutral affect AND foods include sauces or dips THEN do not dip BECAUSE the observed working meal with ketchup avoided dipping, consistent with reducing mess while occupied. IF working context AND neutral affect AND foods without clear dipping use, such as bite-sized sandwiches, THEN do not dip BECAUSE the other working meal also selected no dipping. IF social setting AND neutral affect AND foods include sauces or dips such as ketchup THEN less BECAUSE the social meal selected some dipping but reduced amount, consistent with allowing flavor while limiting mess in front of another person.

wait_before_autocontinue_seconds.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN 100 sec BECAUSE the observed personal meal used a longer wait, suggesting the user wants more time to respond when not rushed by another task. IF social setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN 100 sec BECAUSE the social meal also used the longer wait, consistent with allowing more time when attention may be shared. IF working context AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN 10 sec BECAUSE working meals used the shortest wait, consistent with strong efficiency preferences while occupied.
